1: # Walkthrough: Grown-up Gate PIN Security Implementation
2: 
3: We have successfully implemented and verified the missing parent Gate PIN hashing, settings configuration UI, dialog verification keypad, and automated unit tests to fully comply with **Revision 8 (PIN & key handling)** of the approved revisions brief.
4: 
5: ---
6: 
7: ## 1. Summary of Completed PIN Security Changes
8: 
9: ### Hashing of Gate PINs (`BackupUtils.kt` & `SettingsScreen.kt`)
10: - **Salted Hashing:** Added a `hashPin` utility using `PBKDF2WithHmacSHA256` KDF with **10,000 iterations** and a 256-bit key length.
11: - **Random Salts:** Each time a PIN is set or changed, a random 16-byte salt is generated via `SecureRandom` and encoded alongside the hash.
12: - **Database Storage:** The PIN is stored in the database settings table under the key `"parent_pin"` in the secure format `"hex(salt):hex(hash)"` — it is never written or stored in plaintext.
13: - **Verification Logic:** Added a `verifyPin` helper to extract the salt, re-compute the hash for the entered candidate PIN, and verify it against the stored hash.
14: 
15: ### Settings Configuration UI (`SettingsScreen.kt`)
16: - **Security Card:** Added a new settings card titled **"Grown-up Gate Security"** containing:
17:   - **Require Gate PIN switch:** Toggles the `parent_pin_required` configuration. If enabled and no PIN exists, it automatically opens the setup PIN dialog.
18:   - **Set/Change PIN button:** Displays whether a PIN is configured or not, and opens the change PIN dialog.
19: - **Passcode Input Dialog:** Pr
<truncated 264 bytes>
2 seconds transitions the dialog to a **PIN Entry screen** instead of opening the dashboard directly.
23: - **Premium Keypad UI:** Displays:
24:   - 4 circular indicators that fill as numbers are pressed.
25:   - A friendly circular numeric keypad grid (1-9, Clear/Cancel, 0, Backspace).
26:   - An error message display ("Incorrect PIN") with a **visual horizontal wobble/shake animation** on the dots when an incorrect code is entered.
27: 
28: ### Usage Sites Integration (`TodayScreen.kt` & `JarsScreen.kt`)
29: - Updated both `TodayScreen.kt` (for parent corner transition) and `JarsScreen.kt` (for kid coin spending authorization) to collect `pinRequired` and `parentPin` from database settings and pass them to the `GrownUpGateDialog`.
30: 
31: ---
32: 
33: ## 2. Automated Unit Tests
34: 
35: A new test file [PinSecurityTest.kt](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/ParentingSophia/SophiaQuest/app/src/test/java/com/example/sophiaquest/PinSecurityTest.kt) has been added to verify:
36: - `testPinHashing()`: Validates correct format ("saltHex:hashHex"), hex lengths, and that the plaintext PIN is never exposed.
37: - `testPinVerification()`: Asserts that verification succeeds with the correct PIN and fails for incorrect, empty, or non-numeric PIN inputs.
38: - `testDifferentSaltsProduceDifferentHashes()`: Verifies that hashing the same PIN with different salts yields completely different stored values, preventing rainbow-table precomputations.
39: 
40: ---
41: 
42: ## 3. Test Verification Output
43: 
44: A clean build and test suite run verified all tests compile and pass successfully:
45: ```
46: BUILD SUCCESSFUL in 39s
47: 29 actionable tasks: 29 executed
48: ```
49: 
50: ### Verified Test Suites:
51: 1. `PinSecurityTest` (3 tests passed)
52: 2. `InvariantsTest` (2 tests passed)
53: 3. `TtsSanitizerTest` (2 tests passed)
54: 4. `SophiaQuestRepositoryTest` (9 tests passed)
55: Total passing tests: **16 unit tests**.

Plaintext PINs are saved in settings (`parent_pin`).\\n2. There is no verification of the PIN when traversing the Grown-up Gate (only the 2-second hold).\\n3. There is no UI in `SettingsScreen.kt` for the parent to configure or toggle the Gate PIN.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Proposed Changes\\n\\n### Component 1: Security Utilities & Repository\\n\\n#### [MODIFY] [BackupUtils.kt](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/ParentingSophia/SophiaQuest/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaquest/utils/BackupUtils.kt)\\n- Add Kotlin-only hex encoding/decoding helper functions to avoid dependency on Android frameworks inside unit tests.\\n- Add `hashPin(pin: String, salt: ByteArray): String` which uses `PBKDF2WithHmacSHA256` with 10,000 iterations and a 256-bit key length to return a hex-encoded hash of the 4-digit PIN.\\n- Add `verifyPin(enteredPin: String, storedPinSettingValue: String): Boolean` to check a candidate PIN. The setting value will be stored in the format `\\\"hex(salt):hex(hash)\\\"`.\\n- Add `generateSalt(): ByteArray` returning a random 16-byte salt.\\n\\n#### [MODIFY] [SettingsScreen.kt (ViewModel)](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/ParentingSophia/SophiaQuest/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaquest/ui/parent/SettingsScreen.kt)\\n- Update `setParentPin(pin: String)` in `SettingsViewModel` to generate a salt, hash the PIN, and save the result as `\\\"hex(salt):hex(hash)\\\"`.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Component 2: UI Screens & Gating\\n\\n#### [MODIFY] [SettingsScreen.kt (UI)](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/ParentingSophia/SophiaQuest/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaquest/ui/parent/SettingsScreen.kt)\\n- Add a new settings card category\n<truncated 3085 bytes>",

  "CodeContent": "\"# Walkthrough: Grown-up Gate PIN Security Implementation\\n\\nWe have successfully implemented and verified the missing parent Gate PIN hashing, settings configuration UI, dialog verification keypad, and automated unit tests to fully comply with **Revision 8 (PIN & key handling)** of the approved revisions brief.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 1. Enforces numeric-only inputs and exactly 4 digits.\\n\\n### Grown-up Gate dialog with PIN Pad (`GrownUpGate.kt`)\\n- **Keypad Transition:** If `pinRequired` is true, successfully holding the gate button for 2 seconds transitions the dialog to a **PIN Entry screen** instead of opening the dashboard directly.\\n- **Premium Keypad UI:** Displays:\\n  - 4 circular indicators that fill as numbers are pressed.\\n  - A friendly circular numeric keypad grid (1-9, Clear/C\n<truncated 1614 bytes>",

9: ### A gamified, parent-and-child chore-tracking and money-sense Android app for a kindergartener

11: > **Document purpose:** Hand this to whoever builds the app (currently planned as a solo / AI-assisted build) so the goals, users, UI/UX constraints, asset pipeline, architecture, and build sequence are clear. PRD + project plan with milestones, tasks, and dependencies.

<truncated 45009 bytes>

487: **Research grounding (companion artifact):** chores build executive function/confidence (La Trobe, *Australian Occupational Therapy Journal*, 2022 — correlational); routine/contribution/calm parent time are protective during divorce (AAP, Cleveland Clinic, Child Mind Institute); keep base allowance separate from chores, pay only for extra jobs (Lieber; T. Rowe Price); overjustification effect is real (Lepper, Greene & Nisbett, 1973) -> reward-fade + contribution language; tangible cash beats screen numbers at age 5 -> physical jars + reconciliation; abstract interest too advanced at 5 (CFPB) -> **save-goal bonus instead of compounding in v1**; music-based brushing + visual timers help (Brush DJ; Time Timer).

Pixel 10 Pro — 6.3" LTPO OLED, 1280x2856, centered punch-hole, Android 16, Tensor G5 (GSMArena review, Sept 2025). 1: # Sophia's Quest: Comprehensive Code Audit & Security Review
2: **Author: Senior Code Reviewer**  
3: **Date: June 9, 2026**
4: 
5: ---
6: 
7: ## Executive Summary
8: After a thorough audit of the code delivered by the development team for **Sophia's Quest**, several critical architectural flaws, security gaps, and functional bugs have been uncovered. Left unaddressed, these issues would result in gate bypasses by children, database concurrency race conditions, date-handling bugs, and broken user flows.
9: 
10: This report outlines the identified issues and provides concrete remediation steps.
11: 
12: ---
13: 
14: ## 1. Identified Vulnerabilities & Quality Issues
15: 
16: ### Issue 1: Grown-up Gate Bypass (Security Vulnerability)
17: * **File:** [GrownUpGate.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/ParentingSophia/SophiaQuest/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaquest/ui/components/GrownUpGate.kt) & [TodayScreen.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/ParentingSophia/SophiaQuest/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaquest/ui/kid/TodayScreen.kt)
18: * **Description:** While the Settings screen allows the parent to enable a 4-digit parent PIN and save it to `parent_pin`, the `GrownUpGateDialog` has no logic to check or enforce this PIN. 154:         val factory = SecretKeyFactory.getInstance(KEY_DERIVATION_ALGORITHM)
155:         val spec = PBEKeySpec(pin.toCharArray(), salt, 10000, KEY_LENGTH)
156:         val key = factory.generateSecret(spec)
157:         val hashBytes = key.encoded
158:         return "${salt.toHex()}:${hashBytes.toHex()}"

161:     fun verifyPin(enteredPin: String, storedPinSettingValue: String): Boolean {
162:         return try {
163:             val parts = storedPinSettingValue.split(":")

166:             val expectedHash = parts[1].hexToByteArray()

175:             false

18:

23:

31:         return runCatching {
32:             val logFile = File(context.filesDir, LOG_FILE_NAME)

34:         }.getOrElse { "Error reading logs: ${it.localizedMessage}" }
35:     }
36:

38:         runCatching {

## 🤖 Assistant (2026-06-09 23:49:41 UTC)

Based on the project requirements and the code current state, here is what is next on the build plan:

### 1. Developer Diagnostic Logging (PRD §6.12 & Revision 11)
While the `DevLogger` utility has been scaffolded, it is not yet integrated into the runtime flow.
* **App Instrumentation**: Add `DevLogger.log()` calls across key operations:
  * **MainActivity / Application lifecycle** (e.g., startup, onboarding status checks).
  * **Navigation transitions** (switching between the Kid Surface and Grown-up Corner).
  * **Kid activities** (chore completion, undo completion, toothbrushing timer triggers).
  * **Ledger modifications** (spending entries, Sunday allowance splits, extra-job commissions).
  * **Database actions** (backup export/restores, process restarts, errors and stack traces).
* **Debug-Only Diagnostics Screen**:
  * Create a simple debug screen to view `DevLogger.getLogContent()`.
  * Add buttons to **Share/Export** (via `Intent.createChooser` for sharing the text file) and **Clear** logs.
  * Register the screen in the navigation graph and expose an entry point in the Parent Settings under a `BuildConfig.DEBUG` check so it is stripped/disabled in release builds.

### 2. Local Notifications (PRD §6.8)
No notification engine exists yet.
* **WorkManager / AlarmManager Integration**: Implement local on-device scheduling (no network/push services) for:
  * A morning/bedtime routine reminder.
  * A weekly reminder prompt for the "Jar-Check" reconciliation.
* **Parent Setting Controls**: Wire these to settings toggles in the parent dashboard so they remain **off by default and strictly opt-in**.

### 3. Safe-Area & Accessibility Audit (PRD §5.8 & §7.3)
Perform visual verification and adjustment for the two target physical screen ratios:
* **Pixel 10 Pro XL (6.8") & Pixel 10 Pro (6.3")**:
  * Verify that edge-to-edge layouts correctly handle `WindowInsets` and `DisplayCutout`.
  * Ensure that header text, navigation buttons, the mascot's face, and interactive elements are clear of the top-centered camera punch-hole and system nav bars.
  * Assert that all kid action targets are at least **64dp** for motor-skill compatibility.

---

### Phase 6: Cross-Household Sync (v1.1)
* Once the local v1 pilot with Sophia is completed and verified, Phase 6 will be unlocked **only if the co-parent (Amy) opts in** to participate, enabling the Laragon self-hosted family API and DB sync mechanism.

---

922: fun lerpColor(c1: Color, c2: Color, fraction: Float): Color {
923:     val f = fraction.coerceIn(0f, 1f)
924:     return Color(
925:         red = c1.red + (c2.red - c1.red) * f,
926:         green = c1.green + (c2.green - c1.green) * f,
927:         blue = c1.blue + (c2.blue - c1.blue) * f,
928:         alpha = c1.alpha + (c2.alpha - c1.alpha) * f
929:     )
930: }
931: 
932: fun getBackgroundGradient(fraction: Float): Brush {
933:     val (startColor, endColor) = when {
934:         fraction < 0.33f -> {
935:             val localFraction = fraction / 0.33f
936:             Pair(
937:                 lerpColor(Color(0xFFFFA726), Color(0xFF29B6F6), localFraction),
938:                 lerpColor(Color(0xFFFFEB3B), Color(0xFFB3E5FC), localFraction)
939:             )
940:         }
941:         fraction < 0.66f -> {
942:             val localFraction = (fraction - 0.33f) / 0.33f
943:             Pair(
944:                 lerpColor(Color(0xFF29B6F6), Color(0xFFEC407A), localFraction),
945:                 lerpColor(Color(0xFFB3E5FC), Color(0xFFFF7043), localFraction)
946:             )
947:         }
948:         else -> {
949:             val localFraction = ((fraction - 0.66f) / 0.34f).coerceIn(0f, 1f)
950:             Pair(
951:                 lerpColor(Color(0xFFEC407A), Color(0xFF1A237E), localFraction),
952:                 lerpColor(Color(0xFFFF7043), Color(0xFF0D47A1), localFraction)
953:             )
954:         }
955:     }
956:     return Brush.verticalGradient(listOf(startColor, endColor))
957: }

1: # Implementation Plan: Hashing and Enforcing Grown-up Gate PIN
2: 
3: This plan details the steps to fully implement **Revision 8 (PIN & key handling)** and **Revision 1** from [sophias-quest-antigravity-plan-revisions.md](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/ParentingSophia/docs/sophias-quest-antigravity-plan-revisions.md).
4: 
5: Currently:
6: 1. Plaintext PINs are saved in settings (`parent_pin`).
7: 2. There is no verification of the PIN when traversing the Grown-up Gate (only the 2-second hold).
8: 3. There is no UI in `SettingsScreen.kt` for the parent to configure or toggle the Gate PIN.
9: 
10: ---
11: 
12: ## Proposed Changes
13: 
14: ### Component 1: Security Utilities & Repository
15: 
16: #### [MODIFY] [BackupUtils.kt](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/ParentingSophia/SophiaQuest/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaquest/utils/BackupUtils.kt)
17: - Add Kotlin-only hex encoding/decoding helper functions to avoid dependency on Android frameworks inside unit tests.
18: - Add `hashPin(pin: String, salt: ByteArray): String` which uses `PBKDF2WithHmacSHA256` with 10,000 iterations and a 256-bit key length to return a hex-encoded hash of the 4-digit PIN.
19: - Add `verifyPin(enteredPin: String, storedPinSettingValue: String): Boolean` to check a candidate PIN. The setting value will be stored in the format `"hex(salt):hex(hash)"`.
20: - Add `generateSalt(): ByteArray` returning a random 16-byte salt.
21: 
22: #### [MODIFY] [SettingsScreen.kt (ViewModel)](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%
<truncated 1812 bytes>
 digits are input, call `BackupUtils.verifyPin`. If correct, trigger `onGatePassed()`. If incorrect, trigger a visual wobble animation on the dots, show "Incorrect PIN. Try again.", and clear the entry.
52: 
53: #### [MODIFY] [TodayScreen.kt](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/ParentingSophia/SophiaQuest/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaquest/ui/kid/TodayScreen.kt)
54: - Collect `pinRequired` and `parentPin` from the repository and pass them to the `GrownUpGateDialog`.
55: 
56: #### [MODIFY] [JarsScreen.kt](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/ParentingSophia/SophiaQuest/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaquest/ui/kid/JarsScreen.kt)
57: - Collect `pinRequired` and `parentPin` from the repository and pass them to the `GrownUpGateDialog` triggered during spending actions.
58: 
59: ---
60: 
61: ## Verification Plan
62: 
63: ### Automated Tests
64: - Add a new test suite `PinSecurityTest.kt` under `app/src/test/java/com/example/sophiaquest/`:
65:   - `testPinHashing()`: Verify that a raw PIN is hashed, that different PINs or salts produce different hashes, and that no plaintext remains visible.
66:   - `testPinVerification()`: Assert that verification succeeds with the correct PIN and fails for incorrect PINs or empty strings.
67:   - Run all unit tests: `.\gradlew testDebugUnitTest`.
68: 
69: ### Manual Verification
70: 1. Open settings, verify the **"Grown-up Gate PIN Security"** card is present.
71: 2. Toggle on "Require PIN for Grown-up Zone", enter a new 4-digit PIN, and save it.
72: 3. Check the database or verify using settings logs that it is stored as a hashed string.
73: 4. Try to access the parent corner from `TodayScreen` or spend coins from `JarsScreen`.
74: 5. Hold the gate button for 2 seconds. Verify that the PIN pad displays.
75: 6. Enter an incorrect PIN. Verify that the dots shake/wobble and clear.
76: 7. Enter the correct PIN. Verify that access is successfully granted.

921: 
922: fun lerpColor(c1: Color, c2: Color, fraction: Float): Color {
923:     val f = fraction.coerceIn(0f, 1f)
924:     return Color(
925:         red = c1.red + (c2.red - c1.red) * f,
926:         green = c1.green + (c2.green - c1.green) * f,
927:         blue = c1.blue + (c2.blue - c1.blue) * f,
928:         alpha = c1.alpha + (c2.alpha - c1.alpha) * f
929:     )
930: }
931: 
932: fun getBackgroundGradient(fraction: Float): Brush {
933:     val (startColor, endColor) = when {
934:         fraction < 0.33f -> {
935:             val localFraction = fraction / 0.33f
936:             Pair(
937:                 lerpColor(Color(0xFFFFA726), Color(0xFF29B6F6), localFraction),
938:                 lerpColor(Color(0xFFFFEB3B), Color(0xFFB3E5FC), localFraction)
939:             )
940:         }
941:         fraction < 0.66f -> {
942:             val localFraction = (fraction - 0.33f) / 0.33f
943:             Pair(
944:                 lerpColor(Color(0xFF29B6F6), Color(0xFFEC407A), localFraction),
945:                 lerpColor(Color(0xFFB3E5FC), Color(0xFFFF7043), localFraction)
946:             )
947:         }
948:         else -> {
949:             val localFraction = ((fraction - 0.66f) / 0.34f).coerceIn(0f, 1f)
950:             Pair(
951:                 lerpColor(Color(0xFFEC407A), Color(0xFF1A237E), localFraction),
952:                 lerpColor(Color(0xFFFF7043), Color(0xFF0D47A1), localFraction)
953:             )
954:         }
955:     }
956:     return Brush.verticalGradient(listOf(startColor, endColor))
957: }
958:

1: # Implementation Plan: Hashing and Enforcing Grown-up Gate PIN
2: 
3: This plan details the steps to fully implement **Revision 8 (PIN & key handling)** and **Revision 1** from [sophias-quest-antigravity-plan-revisions.md](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/ParentingSophia/docs/sophias-quest-antigravity-plan-revisions.md).
4: 
5: Currently:
6: 1. Plaintext PINs are saved in settings (`parent_pin`).
7: 2. There is no verification of the PIN when traversing the Grown-up Gate (only the 2-second hold).
8: 3. There is no UI in `SettingsScreen.kt` for the parent to configure or toggle the Gate PIN.
9: 
10: ---
11: 
12: ## Proposed Changes
13: 
14: ### Component 1: Security Utilities & Repository
15: 
16: #### [MODIFY] [BackupUtils.kt](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/ParentingSophia/SophiaQuest/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaquest/utils/BackupUtils.kt)
17: - Add Kotlin-only hex encoding/decoding helper functions to avoid dependency on Android frameworks inside unit tests.
18: - Add `hashPin(pin: String, salt: ByteArray): String` which uses `PBKDF2WithHmacSHA256` with 10,000 iterations and a 256-bit key length to return a hex-encoded hash of the 4-digit PIN.
19: - Add `verifyPin(enteredPin: String, storedPinSettingValue: String): Boolean` to check a candidate PIN. The setting value will be stored in the format `"hex(salt):hex(hash)"`.
20: - Add `generateSalt(): ByteArray` returning a random 16-byte salt.
21: 
22: #### [MODIFY] [SettingsScreen.kt (ViewModel)](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Document
<truncated 1802 bytes>
 digits are input, call `BackupUtils.verifyPin`. If correct, trigger `onGatePassed()`. If incorrect, trigger a visual wobble animation on the dots, show "Incorrect PIN. Try again.", and clear the entry.
52: 
53: #### [MODIFY] [TodayScreen.kt](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/ParentingSophia/SophiaQuest/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaquest/ui/kid/TodayScreen.kt)
54: - Collect `pinRequired` and `parentPin` from the repository and pass them to the `GrownUpGateDialog`.
55: 
56: #### [MODIFY] [JarsScreen.kt](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/ParentingSophia/SophiaQuest/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaquest/ui/kid/JarsScreen.kt)
57: - Collect `pinRequired` and `parentPin` from the repository and pass them to the `GrownUpGateDialog` triggered during spending actions.
58: 
59: ---
60: 
61: ## Verification Plan
62: 
63: ### Automated Tests
64: - Add a new test suite `PinSecurityTest.kt` under `app/src/test/java/com/example/sophiaquest/`:
65:   - `testPinHashing()`: Verify that a raw PIN is hashed, that different PINs or salts produce different hashes, and that no plaintext remains visible.
66:   - `testPinVerification()`: Assert that verification succeeds with the correct PIN and fails for incorrect PINs or empty strings.
67:   - Run all unit tests: `.\gradlew testDebugUnitTest`.
68: 
69: ### Manual Verification
70: 1. Open settings, verify the **"Grown-up Gate PIN Security"** card is present.
71: 2. Toggle on "Require PIN for Grown-up Zone", enter a new 4-digit PIN, and save it.
72: 3. Check the database or verify using settings logs that it is stored as a hashed string.
73: 4. Try to access the parent corner from `TodayScreen` or spend coins from `JarsScreen`.
74: 5. Hold the gate button for 2 seconds. Verify that the PIN pad displays.
75: 6. Enter an incorrect PIN. Verify that the dots shake/wobble and clear.
76: 7. Enter the correct PIN. Verify that access is successfully granted.
77:

8: # Plan Review Response — Approved With Required Revisions
9: 
10: Your Implementation Plan is approved to proceed **after** you apply the revisions below. Re-output the corrected module map + Room schema (incorporating these), then scaffold the foundation. If any revision conflicts with an invariant from the Build Brief, stop and flag it rather than guessing.
11: 
12: ## Keep unchanged (these are correct — do not alter)
13: - The `Stars` / `Cents` type wall as `@JvmInline value class` with **no conversion function anywhere**.
14: - The sealed positive-only `MascotState` (Content / Playful / Excited / Sleepy; no negative state representable).
15: - The append-only `ledger_entries` table with balances derived via `SUM()` (never an in-place balance column).
16: - The unique index on `task_completions(taskId, completionDate)`.
17: - `android:allowBackup="false"` in the manifest.
18: - The data / ui(kid,parent,components) / utils module structure.
19: 
20: ---
21: 
22: ## 1. Storage location & backup encryption (DECISION: everything lives in Dropbox)
23: 
24: The owner has chosen to keep the entire project — source and exported backups — inside a Dropbox-synced folder. Implement accordingly, with these hard rules:
25: 
26: - **The app integrates NO cloud SDK.** Do not add Dropbox, Drive, or any cloud client/credentials. The app 
<truncated 9084 bytes>
5: 
136: ## Corrected Room schema (v1, summary of net result)
137: 
138: - `tasks` — id, title, iconName, starReward, isExtraJob, rewardCents, schedule, isArchived
139: - `task_completions` — id, taskId(FK), completionDate, completionType {ROUTINE, COMMISSION}, status {COMPLETED for routine; PENDING_APPROVAL/APPROVED/REJECTED for commission} *(unique index on (taskId, completionDate))*
140: - `ledger_entries` — id, jarType {SPEND,SAVE,GIVE}, amountCents (signed Int), transactionType {ALLOWANCE,COMMISSION,SPENT,RECONCILIATION,BONUS}, approvedCompletionId (FK, non-null on COMMISSION), note, timestamp
141: - `dragon_hatch_progress` — id, dragonName, dragonType, requiredStars, earnedStars, isHatched
142: - `save_goals` — id, name, imageRef, targetCents, createdTimestamp, achievedTimestamp(nullable), isActive
143: - `jar_allocation_versions` — id, version, spendPercent, savePercent, givePercent, activeFromTimestamp
144: - `settings` — key, value (holds onboarding_completed, gate PIN hash, backup-passphrase KDF params, calm_mode, routine/dragon/money layer flags)
145: 
146: ## Updated verification plan
147: - `testTypeSafetyWall()` (reworded), `testMascotStateExclusivity()` (reworded), `testTtsSanitization()` (new), `testLedgerCorrectness()` (unique completion index prevents double star allocation), `testNoCommissionWithoutApproval()` (COMMISSION rows require an approvedCompletionId), `testNoNegativeJarBalance()`, `testBackupRoundTrip()` (WAL checkpoint → encrypt → restore → integrity), `testRollingFrequencyNotStreak()`, `testLayerGatingHidesKidSurfaces()`.
148: 
149: ## How to proceed

151: 2. Scaffold the foundation with the invariant-enforcing types and tests in place.
152: 3. Then build Layer 1 → Layer 2 → Layer 3 per the Build Brief, flagging any feature that would breach an invariant.

1: # Walkthrough: Grown-up Gate PIN Security Implementation
2: 
3: We have successfully implemented and verified the missing parent Gate PIN hashing, settings configuration UI, dialog verification keypad, and automated unit tests to fully comply with **Revision 8 (PIN & key handling)** of the approved revisions brief.
4: 
5: ---
6: 
7: ## 1. Summary of Completed PIN Security Changes
8: 
9: ### Hashing of Gate PINs (`BackupUtils.kt` & `SettingsScreen.kt`)
10: - **Salted Hashing:** Added a `hashPin` utility using `PBKDF2WithHmacSHA256` KDF with **10,000 iterations** and a 256-bit key length.
11: - **Random Salts:** Each time a PIN is set or changed, a random 16-byte salt is generated via `SecureRandom` and encoded alongside the hash.
12: - **Database Storage:** The PIN is stored in the database settings table under the key `"parent_pin"` in the secure format `"hex(salt):hex(hash)"` — it is never written or stored in plaintext.
13: - **Verification Logic:** Added a `verifyPin` helper to extract the salt, re-compute the hash for the entered candidate PIN, and verify it against the stored hash.
14: 
15: ### Settings Configuration UI (`SettingsScreen.kt`)
16: - **Security Card:** Added a new settings card titled **"Grown-up Gate Security"** containing:
17:   - **Require Gate PIN switch:** Toggles the `parent_pin_required` configuration. If enabled and no PIN exists, it automatically opens the setup PIN dialog.
18:   - **Set/Change PIN button:** Displays whether a PIN is configured or not, and opens the change PIN dialog.
19: - **Passcode Input Dialog:** Pr
<truncated 264 bytes>
2 seconds transitions the dialog to a **PIN Entry screen** instead of opening the dashboard directly.
23: - **Premium Keypad UI:** Displays:
24:   - 4 circular indicators that fill as numbers are pressed.
25:   - A friendly circular numeric keypad grid (1-9, Clear/Cancel, 0, Backspace).
26:   - An error message display ("Incorrect PIN") with a **visual horizontal wobble/shake animation** on the dots when an incorrect code is entered.
27: 
28: ### Usage Sites Integration (`TodayScreen.kt` & `JarsScreen.kt`)
29: - Updated both `TodayScreen.kt` (for parent corner transition) and `JarsScreen.kt` (for kid coin spending authorization) to collect `pinRequired` and `parentPin` from database settings and pass them to the `GrownUpGateDialog`.
30: 
31: ---
32:

36: - `testPinHashing()`: Validates correct format ("saltHex:hashHex"), hex lengths, and that the plaintext PIN is never exposed.
37: - `testPinVerification()`: Asserts that verification succeeds with the correct PIN and fails for incorrect, empty, or non-numeric PIN inputs.
38: - `testDifferentSaltsProduceDifferentHashes()`: Verifies that hashing the same PIN with different salts yields completely different stored values, preventing rainbow-table precomputations.

  "ArtifactMetadata": "{\"ArtifactType\":\"implementation_plan\",\"RequestFeedback\":true,\"Summary\":\"Implementation plan to fix the dragon egg hatching flow and resolve the star/points counter lag by introducing hatch acknowledgment UI and updating unit tests.\"}",
  "CodeContent": "\"# Implementation Plan: Dragon Hatch Acknowledgment and Star-Count Sync\\n\\nThis plan addresses the bug where the dragon egg reaches the required star count but remains stuck without hatching UI feedback, while subsequent stars are awarded to the next unhatched dragon hidden in the database, causing the UI to appear as if it \\\"lost count of the points through the day.\\\"\\n\\n## Proposed Changes\\n\\n### Component 1: Today Screen UI & ViewModel\\n\\n#### [MODIFY] [TodayScreen.kt](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/ParentingSophia/SophiaQuest/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaquest/ui/kid/TodayScreen.kt)\\n- **ViewModel Updates:**\\n  - Update `TodayUiState.Success` signature to include `val activeDragonId: Long`.\\n  - Inside `TodayViewModel.uiState` flow construction, map `activeDragonId = activeDragon?.id ?: 0L` to `TodayUiState.Success`.\\n  - Add method `fun acknowledgeHatch(dragonId: Long)` to `TodayViewModel` that launches a coroutine calling `repository.acknowledgeDragonHatch(dragonId)`.\\n- **UI Screen Updates:**\\n  - If `state.isHatched` is true in `TodayScreen.kt`, replace the standard star progress bar `Card` with a premium **Celebratory Card**:\\n    - Background: Warm light gold/yellow (`0xFFFFF9C4`).\\n    - Title: `\\\"\ud83c\udf89 [state.activeDragonName] Hatched! \ud83c\udf89\\\"` in dark purple.\\n    - Subtext: `\\\"Your baby [state.activeDragonType] is ready to play in the nook!\\\"`.\\n    - Action Button: `\\\"Get Next Egg! \ud83e\udd5a\\\"` which plays the fanfare sound using `SoundSynth.playFanfare()` and calls `viewModel.acknowledgeHatch(state.activeDragonId)`.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Component 2: Unit Tests\\n\\n#### [MODIFY] [SophiaQuestRepositoryTest.kt](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/ParentingSophia/SophiaQuest/app/src/test/java/com/example/sophiaquest/SophiaQuestRepositoryTest.kt)\\n- **Fix Test Failure:**\\n  - In `testRoutineChoreAwardsStarsAndHatchesDragon()`, once Sparky reaches 10/10 stars and is confirmed to be hatched, call `repository.acknowledgeDragonHatch(sparky.id)` to simulate the child ackno\n<truncated 1110 bytes>",

  "CodeContent": "\"# Implementation Plan: Dragon Hatch Flow & Toothbrushing Parent Bypass\\n\\nThis plan addresses:\\n1. Tapping "Get Next Egg!" acknowledges the hatch and transitions the active dragon to the next unhatched egg. Any stars earned in the background are correctly preserved and displayed on the new egg.
* **Teeth Brushing Bypass:** Added a gesture listener on the dragon mascot in [ToothbrushingScreen.kt](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/ParentingSophia/SophiaQuest/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaquest/ui/kid/ToothbrushingScreen.kt). ## 🤖 Assistant (2026-06-10 16:23:17 UTC)

Here is a copy-pasteable prompt you can give to Claude. It instructs Claude to act as a music supervisor and generate tailored **Style Tags**, **Prompt Details**, and **Structure Guides** for Suno AI to produce the exact tracks needed for the app:

***

```markdown
Act as an expert Music Supervisor and Suno AI prompting specialist. I am building "Sophia's Quest", a gamified Android chore and habit-tracking app for a 5-year-old. The app has a playful, magical, retro-arcade aesthetic (drawing inspiration from classic 8-bit and 16-bit chiptune video game music, but kept warm, soothing, and kid-friendly).

I need you to generate precise Suno AI prompt parameters (specifically: Style of Music, Lyrics/Structure guidelines, and Title ideas) for the following 4 necessary tracks in our app:

1. "Sparkling Teeth" (Toothbrushing Timer Track)
   - Purpose: Plays during the 2-minute toothbrushing countdown. Needs to keep a 5-year-old engaged, dancing, and brushing at a steady rhythm.
   - Vibe: Energetic, bubbly, bubbly water sounds, rhythmic, clean, happy, bright.

2. "Cosy Dragon Nook" (Playroom/Nook Screen Background Music)
   - Purpose: Ambient loop playing while the child interacts with baby dragons in their nook.
   - Vibe: Peaceful, magical, sleepy, warm, cozy, calming lofi chiptune. No sudden loud noises (sensory friendly).

3. "Morning Quest" (Today Screen/Adventure Path Loop)
   - Purpose: Plays while the child scrolls the rainbow path and checks off morning chores.
   - Vibe: Playful, adventurous, walking-tempo, classic retro-game stage start, encouraging, sunny.

4. "Dragon Hatch Fanfare" (Celebration & Hatching Sound)
   - Purpose: A short, high-impact celebratory loop (5 to 10 seconds) that plays when an egg hatches or a save goal is reached.
   - Vibe: Triumphant, sparkling, magical fanfare, victory chime, 8-bit synth blast.

For each track, please provide:
1. Suno Style Tags: (Max 120 characters, comma-separated keywords that Suno's algorithm understands best, e.g., "8-bit, chiptune, warm synth, upbeat, 120bpm")
2. Prompt Description: (The main prompt text to input into Suno's "Style of Music" box to get the right instrumentation and tempo)
3. Lyrics/Structure instructions: (Whether it should be [Instrumental], what structure tags like [Drop], [Intro], [Main Loop] to use to get the cleanest loopable track)
4. Tips for looping: How to edit the resulting track so it loops seamlessly in an Android app.
```

## 👤 User Request (2026-06-10 16:30:10 UTC)

whats the best download method - mp3, wav, stems?

---

## 🤖 Assistant (2026-06-10 16:30:10 UTC)

The best download method is to download the **WAV** file first, and then convert it for the app. Here is why:

### 1. Why Download WAV from Suno?
* **Maximum Quality:** WAV is uncompressed lossy-free audio. Downloading the WAV gives you the clean, high-fidelity master track.
* **Flexibility:** You can easily crop, edit, and convert a WAV file without losing any quality. (Stems are only necessary if you want to remix or isolate individual instruments like drums or bass in a digital audio workstation).

---

### 2. Best Format to import into the Android App: **OGG Vorbis (`.ogg`)**
While you might be tempted to put an MP3 directly in the app, **MP3 is the worst format for loops** due to a technical limitation:
* **The MP3 Loop Gap:** MP3 encoders automatically add a tiny, silent padding gap (approx. 20–50ms) at the beginning and end of files. This causes a noticeable "hiccup" or click every time the timer music loops.
* **OGG is designed for loops:** The `.ogg` format supports **true gapless looping** and compiles to a very small file size (around 1–2 MB for a 2-minute track), keeping your APK lightweight.

---

### 💡 Recommended Workflow:
1. Download the **WAV** from Suno.
2. Open it in a free audio tool like **Audacity** (or an online converter).
3. Trim it so the start and end match up cleanly for a loop.
4. Export it as an **OGG Vorbis (`.ogg`)** file at `96kbps` or `128kbps` quality.
5. Drop the `.ogg` file directly into the Android project's `res/raw/` directory.

---

9: object SoundSynth {
10:     private const val SAMPLE_RATE = 22050
11: 
12:     /**
13:      * Short 8-bit bubble pop sound for button taps.
14:      */
15:     fun playPop() {
16:         val durationMs = 60
17:         val numSamples = (SAMPLE_RATE * (durationMs / 1000.0)).toInt()
18:         val buffer = ShortArray(numSamples)
19:         for (i in 0 until numSamples) {
20:             val t = i.toDouble() / SAMPLE_RATE
21:             // Freq sweeps rapidly down from 500Hz to 100Hz
22:             val freq = 500.0 - (400.0 * (i.toDouble() / numSamples))
23:             val angle = 2.0 * Math.PI * freq * t
24:             val envelope = 1.0 - (i.toDouble() / numSamples)
25:             buffer[i] = (sin(angle) * 16383.0 * envelope).toInt().toShort()
26:         }
27:         playPcm(buffer)
28:     }
29: 
30:     /**
31:      * Joyful rising C-major arpeggio chime for task completion / star reward.

34:         val durationMs = 320

38:             val t = i.toDouble() / SAMPLE_RATE
39:             // Arpeggio notes: C5 (523Hz), E5 (659Hz), G5 (784Hz), C6 (1046Hz)
40:

toShort()

123: 
124:     fun playMusicNote(freq: Double, durationMs: Int) {
125:         val numSamples = (SAMPLE_RATE * (durationMs / 1000.0)).toInt()
126:         val buffer = ShortArray(numSamples)
127:         for (i in 0 until numSamples) {
128:             val t = i.toDouble() / SAMPLE_RATE
129:             val angle = 2.0 * Math.PI * freq * t
130:             // Retro square/triangle wave
131:             val raw = if (sin(angle) > 0) 1.0 else -1.0
132:             val envelope = 1.0 - (i.toDouble() / numSamples) * 0.2
133:             buffer[i] = (raw * 4000.0 * envelope).toInt().toShort()
134:         }
135:         playPcm(buffer)
136:     }
137: 
138:     private fun playPcm(buffer: ShortArray) {
139:         val currentScale = volumeScale
140:         val scaledBuffer = ShortArray(buffer.size)
141:         for (i in buffer.indices) {
142:             scaledBuffer[i] = (buffer[i] * currentScale).toInt().coerceIn(-32768, 32767).toShort()
143:         }
144: 
145:         Thread {
146:             try {
147:                 val track = AudioTrack(
148:                     AudioManager.STREAM_MUSIC,
149:                     SAMPLE_RATE,
150:                     AudioFormat.CHANNEL_OUT_MONO,
151:                     AudioFormat.ENCODING_PCM_16BIT,
152:                     scaledBuffer.size * 2,
153:                     AudioTrack.MODE_STATIC
154:                 )
155:                 track.write(scaledBuffer, 0, scaledBuffer.size)
156:                 track.play()
157:                 val playDurationMs = (scaledBuffer.size * 1000L) / SAMPLE_RATE
158:                 Thread.sleep(playDurationMs + 50)
159:                 track.stop()
160:                 track.release()
161:             } catch (e: Exception) {
162:                 e.printStackTrace()
163:             }
164:         }.start()
165:     }

Arguments:
```json
{
  "ArtifactMetadata": "{\"ArtifactType\":\"other\",\"RequestFeedback\":false,\"Summary\":\"Updated Python music converter script using chunked writes to avoid libsndfile DLL crashes on Windows.\"}",
  "CodeContent": "\"import os\\nimport soundfile as sf\\n\\nres_raw_dir = r\\\"c:\\\\Users\\\\thoma\\\\Dropbox\\\\My Documents\\\\Programs\\\\ParentingSophia\\\\SophiaQuest\\\\app\\\\src\\\\main\\\\res\\\\raw\\\"\\nos.makedirs(res_raw_dir, exist_ok=True)\\n\\nmusic_dir = r\\\"c:\\\\Users\\\\thoma\\\\Dropbox\\\\My Documents\\\\Programs\\\\ParentingSophia\\\\music\\\"\\nsubdirs = {\\n    \\\"cosy dragon nook\\\": \\\"nook\\\",\\n    \\\"dragon hatch fanfare\\\": \\\"fanfare\\\",\\n    \\\"morning quest\\\": \\\"quest\\\",\\n    \\\"sparkling teeth\\\": \\\"teeth\\\"\\n}\\n\\nfor subdir, prefix in subdirs.items():\\n    path = os.path.join(music_dir, subdir)\\n    if not os.path.exists(path):\\n        print(f\\\"Directory {path} not found.\\\")\\n        continue\\n    \\n    files = [f for f in os.listdir(path) if f.endswith(\\\".wav\\\")]\\n    files.sort()\\n    \\n    print(f\\\"Found {len(files)} files in {subdir}\\\")\\n    for idx, filename in enumerate(files):\\n        wav_path = os.path.join(path, filename)\\n        out_name = f\\\"{prefix}_{idx + 1}.ogg\\\"\\n        out_path = os.path.join(res_raw_dir, out_name)\\n        \\n        print(f\\\"Converting {filename} -> {out_name}...\\\")\\n        try:\\n            data, sr = sf.read(wav_path)\\n            channels = data.shape[1] if len(data.shape) > 1 else 1\\n            chunk_size = sr * 2\\n            with sf.SoundFile(out_path, mode='w', samplerate=sr, channels=channels, format='OGG') as f:\\n                for start in range(0, len(data), chunk_size):\\n                    f.write(data[start:start+chunk_size])\\n        except Exception as e:\\n            print(f\\\"Error converting {filename}: {e}\\\")\\n\\nprint(\\\"All conversions complete!\\\")\\n\"",
  "Description": "\"Overwrite convert_music.py with chunked write method.\"",
  "IsArtifact": "false",
  "Overwrite": "true",
  "TargetFile": "\"c:\\\\Users\\\\thoma\\\\Dropbox\\\\My Documents\\\\Programs\\\\ParentingSophia\\\\docs\\\\convert_music.py\"",
  "toolAction": "\"Write updated converter script\"",
  "toolSummary": "\"Writing updated converter script\""
}

= null\\n    private var currentCategory: String? = null\\n    private var lastTrackIndex: Int = -1\\n\\n    private val nookTracks = listOf(\\n        R.raw.nook_1, R.raw.nook_2, R.raw.nook_3, R.raw.nook_4,\\n        R.raw.nook_5, R.raw.nook_6, R.raw.nook_7, R.raw.nook_8\\n    )\\n\\n    private val fanfareTracks = listOf(\\n        R.raw.fanfare_1, R.raw.fanfare_2, R.raw.fanfare_3, R.raw.fanfare_4,\\n        R.raw.fanfare_5, R.raw.fanfare_6, R.raw.fanfare_7, R.raw.fanfare_8,\\n        R.raw.fanfare_9, R.raw.fanfare_10\\n    )\\n\\n    private val questTracks = listOf(\\n        R.raw.quest_1, R.raw.quest_2, R.raw.quest_3, R.raw.quest_4,\\n        R.raw.quest_5, R.raw.quest_6, R.raw.quest_7, R.raw.quest_8\\n    )\\n\\n    private val teethTracks = listOf(\\n        R.raw.teeth_1, R.raw.teeth_2, R.raw.teeth_3, R.raw.teeth_4,\\n        R.raw.teeth_5, R.raw.teeth_6, R.raw.teeth_7, R.raw.teeth_8,\\n        R.raw.teeth_9, R.raw.teeth_10, R.raw.teeth_11, R.raw.teeth_12\\n    )\\n\\n    fun playNookMusic(context: Context, volume: Float) {\\n        playCategory(context, \\\"nook\\\", nookTracks, volume)\\n    }\\n\\n    fun playQuestMusic(context: Context, volume: Float) {\\n        playCategory(context, \\\"quest\\\", questTracks, volume)\\n    }\\n\\n    fun playBrushingMusic(context: Context, volume: Float) {\\n        playCategory(context, \\\"teeth\\\", teethTracks, volume)\\n    }\\n\\n    private fun playCategory(context: Context, category: String, tracks: List<Int>, volume: Float) {\\n        stopMusic()\\n        currentCategory = category\\n\\n        val size = tracks.size\\n        if (size == 0) return\\n\\n        var nextIdx = (0 until size).random()\\n        if (size > 1 && nextIdx == lastTrackIndex) {\\n            nextIdx = (nextIdx + 1) % size\\n        }\\n        lastTrackIndex = nextIdx\\n        val selectedResId = tracks[nextIdx]\\n\\n    \n<truncated 1659 bytes>",

123:     fun speakBalance(jarName: String, cents: Int) {
124:         val dollars = cents / 100
125:         val remainingCents = cents % 100
126:         val voiceText = if (cents == 0) {
127:             "Your $jarName jar is empty. Complete extra jobs to earn coins!"
128:         } else if (dollars > 0 && remainingCents > 0) {
129:             "Your $jarName jar has $dollars dollars and $remainingCents cents."
130:         } else if (dollars > 0) {
131:             "Your $jarName jar has $dollars dollars."
132:         } else {

11: 
12:     /**
13:      * Short 8-bit bubble pop sound for button taps.
14:      */
15:     fun playPop() {
16:         val durationMs = 60
17:         val numSamples = (SAMPLE_RATE * (durationMs / 1000.0)).toInt()
18:         val buffer = ShortArray(numSamples)
19:         for (i in 0 until numSamples) {
20:             val t = i.toDouble() / SAMPLE_RATE
21:             // Freq sweeps rapidly down from 500Hz to 100Hz
22:             val freq = 500.0 - (400.0 * (i.toDouble() / numSamples))
23:             val angle = 2.0 * Math.PI * freq * t
24:             val envelope = 1.0 - (i.toDouble() / numSamples)
25:             buffer[i] = (sin(angle) * 16383.0 * envelope).toInt().toShort()
26:         }
27:         playPcm(buffer)
28:     }
29: 
30:     /**
31:      * Joyful rising C-major arpeggio chime for task completion / star reward.

34:         val durationMs = 320
35:         val numSamples = (SAMPLE_RATE * (durationMs / 1000.0)).toInt()
36:         val buffer = ShortArray(numSamples)
37:         for (i in 0 until numSamples) {
38:             val t = i.toDouble() / SAMPLE_RATE
39:             // Arpeggio notes: C5 (523Hz), E5 (659Hz), G5 (784Hz), C6 (1046Hz)
40:

toShort()
118:         }

122:     @Volatile var volumeScale: Float = 1.0f
123: 
124:     fun playMusicNote(freq: Double, durationMs: Int) {
125:         val numSamples = (SAMPLE_RATE * (durationMs / 1000.0)).toInt()
126:         val buffer = ShortArray(numSamples)
127:         for (i in 0 until numSamples) {
128:             val t = i.toDouble() / SAMPLE_RATE
129:             val angle = 2.0 * Math.PI * freq * t
130:             // Retro square/triangle wave
131:             val raw = if (sin(angle) > 0) 1.0 else -1.0
132:             val envelope = 1.0 - (i.toDouble() / numSamples) * 0.2
133:             buffer[i] = (raw * 4000.0 * envelope).toInt().toShort()
134:         }
135:         playPcm(buffer)
136:     }
137: 
138:     private fun playPcm(buffer: ShortArray) {
139:         val currentScale = volumeScale
140:         val scaledBuffer = ShortArray(buffer.size)
141:         for (i in buffer.indices) {
142:             scaledBuffer[i] = (buffer[i] * currentScale).toInt().coerceIn(-32768, 32767).toShort()
143:         }
144: 
145:         Thread {
146:             try {
147:                 val track = AudioTrack(
148:                     AudioManager.STREAM_MUSIC,
149:                     SAMPLE_RATE,
150:                     AudioFormat.CHANNEL_OUT_MONO,
151:                     AudioFormat.ENCODING_PCM_16BIT,
152:                     scaledBuffer.size * 2,
153:                     AudioTrack.MODE_STATIC
154:                 )
155:                 track.write(scaledBuffer, 0, scaledBuffer.size)
156:                 track.play()
157:                 val playDurationMs = (scaledBuffer.size * 1000L) / SAMPLE_RATE
158:                 Thread.sleep(playDurationMs + 50)
159:                 track.stop()
160:                 track.release()
161:             } catch (e: Exception) {
162:                 e.printStackTrace()
163:             }
164:         }.start()
165:     }
166: }

2: import soundfile as sf
3: 
4: res_raw_dir = r"c:\Users\thoma\Dropbox\My Documents\Programs\ParentingSophia\SophiaQuest\app\src\main\res\raw"
5: os.makedirs(res_raw_dir, exist_ok=True)
6: 
7: music_dir = r"c:\Users\thoma\Dropbox\My Documents\Programs\ParentingSophia\music"
8: subdirs = {
9:     "cosy dragon nook": "nook",
10:     "dragon hatch fanfare": "fanfare",
11:     "morning quest": "quest",
12:     "sparkling teeth": "teeth"
13: }
14: 
15: for subdir, prefix in subdirs.items():
16:     path = os.path.join(music_dir, subdir)
17:     if not os.path.exists(path):
18:         print(f"Directory {path} not found.")
19:         continue
20:     
21:     files = [f for f in os.listdir(path) if f.endswith(".wav")]
22:     files.sort()
23:     
24:     print(f"Found {len(files)} files in {subdir}")
25:     for idx, filename in enumerate(files):
26:         wav_path = os.path.join(path, filename)
27:         out_name = f"{prefix}_{idx + 1}.ogg"
28:         out_path = os.path.join(res_raw_dir, out_name)
29:         
30:         print(f"Converting {filename} -> {out_name}...")
31:         try:
32:             data, sr = sf.read(wav_path)
33:             channels = data.shape[1] if len(data.shape) > 1 else 1
34:             chunk_size = sr * 2
35:             with sf.SoundFile(out_path, mode='w', samplerate=sr, channels=channels, format='OGG') as f:

## 👤 User Request (2026-06-10 16:51:52 UTC)

also creating evening quest music for the nighttime choires on recommendation from claude ("Want me to spin up alternate versions of any of these (e.g., a calmer variant of Morning Quest for evening chores), or are you heading into Suno to test this batch first?" me: "yes for the morning quest please" claude: "Here's the evening counterpart — same adventure-path DNA, dialed down for wind-down chores.
Evening Quest (Calm Variant)
Style Tags (117 chars):
16-bit chiptune, gentle game overworld theme, soft warm melody, slow 80 BPM, twilight mood, calm, soothing, kids friendly
Style of Music box:
Gentle 16-bit video game overworld theme, soft warm lead synth, mellow walking bassline, slow 80 BPM, calm twilight mood, peaceful evening feel, light soft percussion, classic retro game night stage music, smooth even dynamics, no dramatic builds, no fast sections, no orchestral elements
Lyrics/Structure: [Instrumental] with:
[Intro]
[Main Theme]
[Main Theme]
[Main Theme]
Dropped the [Bridge] from the morning version — bridges introduce contrast, and contrast is the enemy of a calm evening loop. Three [Main Theme] repeats keeps it self-similar.
What changed and why:

80 BPM instead of 100 — still has forward motion for path-scrolling, but no urgency
"Night stage music" — this is the anchor swap. Retro games have a whole vocabulary of night-level themes (think slower, softer versions of the day theme), and Suno knows it
"Twilight," "peaceful evening" — mood words that pull toward minor-tinged-but-warm, without going full lullaby like Cosy Dragon Nook. You want this distinct from the nook track — this is still quest music, just sleepy quest music
Added "no fast sections" to the exclusion list, kept the rest from the morning version

Looping tip: Same as Morning Quest — cut on a [Main Theme] downbeat, 200-500ms crossfade, export OGG.
One design thought: keeping the same melodic family between morning and evening versions (like games do with day/night themes) would be ideal, but Suno can't reliably reuse a melody across generations. Closest workaround: if a Morning Quest generation hits, use Suno's "Cover" or "Remix" feature on that track with this calmer style prompt — it'll keep more melodic DNA than a fresh generation.")

those have been added to the evening quest folder

  "ReplacementContent": "\"    private val questTracks = listOf(\\n        R.raw.quest_1, R.raw.quest_2, R.raw.quest_3, R.raw.quest_4,\\n        R.raw.quest_5, R.raw.quest_6, R.raw.quest_7, R.raw.quest_8\\n    )\\n\\n    private val eveningTracks = listOf(\\n        R.raw.evening_1, R.raw.evening_2, R.raw.evening_3, R.raw.evening_4,\\n        R.raw.evening_5, R.raw.evening_6, R.raw.evening_7, R.raw.evening_8\\n    )\\n\\n    private val teethTracks = listOf(\\n        R.raw.teeth_1, R.raw.teeth_2, R.raw.teeth_3, R.raw.teeth_4,\\n        R.raw.teeth_5, R.raw.teeth_6, R.raw.teeth_7, R.raw.teeth_8,\\n        R.raw.teeth_9, R.raw.teeth_10, R.raw.teeth_11, R.raw.teeth_12\\n    )\\n\\n    fun playNookMusic(context: Context, volume: Float) {\\n        playCategory(context, \\\"nook\\\", nookTracks, volume)\\n    }\\n\\n    fun playQuestMusic(context: Context, volume: Float) {\\n        val calendar = java.util.Calendar.getInstance()\\n        val hour = calendar.get(java.util.Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY)\\n        val isEvening = hour >= 17 || hour < 5 // 5 PM to 5 AM is evening/sleepy time\\n        if (isEvening) {\\n            playCategory(context, \\\"evening\\\", eveningTracks, volume)\\n        } else {\\n            playCategory(context, \\\"quest\\\", questTracks, volume)\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    fun playBrushingMusic(context: Context, volume: Float) {\\n        playCategory(context, \\\"teeth\\\", teethTracks, volume)\\n    }\"",

1: # Walkthrough: Dragon Hatching Flow, Toothbrushing Bypass, & APK Build
2: 
3: We have successfully resolved the dragon egg hatching flow, implemented the parent-bypass shortcut for toothbrushing, verified all unit tests pass, compiled the debug APK, and deployed the build to the local network share and builds website.
4: 
5: ---
6: 
7: ## 1. Summary of Completed Changes
8: 
9: ### Dragon Hatch Acknowledgment Flow (`TodayScreen.kt`)
10: - **State & ViewModel Updates:** Added `activeDragonId` to `TodayUiState.Success`. Implemented `acknowledgeHatch(dragonId: Long)` in `TodayViewModel` which launches a coroutine calling `repository.acknowledgeDragonHatch(dragonId)`.
11: - **Hatch Celebration Card:** If `state.isHatched` is true in `TodayScreen.kt`, the standard star progress bar card is replaced by a bright gold/yellow celebratory card (`0xFFFFF9C4`). This card displays:
12:   - Header: `"🎉 [state.activeDragonName] Hatched! 🎉"` in dark purple.
13:   - Subtext: `"Your baby [state.activeDragonType] is ready to play in the nook!"`.
14:   - Button: `"Get Next Egg! 🥚"` which plays the fanfare chime and triggers `viewModel.acknowledgeHatch(state.activeDragonId)` to transition the active hatching dragon to the next unhatched egg in the database.
15: 
16: ### Toothbrushing Parent Bypass Easter Egg (`ToothbrushingScreen.kt`)
17: - Added a `pointerInput` gesture listener to the toothbrushing mascot `Box`.
18: - **Long Press Gesture:** Parents can press and hold (long-click) the sleeping or brushing dragon mascot to skip the 2-minute timer:
19:   - Plays the fanfare sound.
20:   - Immediately counts down to 0, sets status to completed, and triggers the Text-to-Speech completion prompt: `"Super job! Your teeth are sparkling clean! You earned a star!"`.
21:   - Marks the task as completed in the database.
22: - **Short Tap Gesture:** Standard single tap reads out the current action status.
23: 
24: ### Unit Tests Update (`SophiaQuestRepositoryTest.kt`)
25: - Modified `testRoutineChoreAwardsStarsAndHatchesDragon` to call `repository.acknowledgeDragonHatch(sparky.id)` right after verifying Sparky is hatched.
26: - This transitions the flow to the next unhatched dragon ("Bubbles"), allowing the final assertions checking that Bubbles is now the active dragon to pass cleanly.
27: 
28: ---
29: 
30: ## 2. Compilation and Delivery
31: 
32: ### Gradle Compilation
33: - Cleaned the build tree: `.\gradlew.bat clean`.
34: - Ran unit tests: `.\gradlew.bat testDebugUnitTest` (All 16 unit tests passed successfully).
35: - Built debug APK: `.\gradlew.bat assembleDebug` (Build succeeded).
36: 
37: ### Distribution & Web Sync
38: - **Target APK Path:** Copied the compiled `app-debug.apk` to [SophiaQuest.apk](file:///Y:/ReynoldsFamily/builds/SophiaQuest.apk).
39: - **Builds Website:** Updated the HTML file [index.html](file:///Y:/ReynoldsFamily/builds/index.html) to list "Sophia's Quest" in the builds grid, complete with features list and download link.

 ---
 
+### Background Music Manager & OGG Loops
+- **Audio Conversion:** Converted all 46 Suno-generated `.wav` files to optimized `.ogg` format to support gapless looping in Android (`res/raw` directory). This includes:
+  - 8 cosy dragon nook loops (`nook_1` to `nook_8`)
+  - 8 morning quest loops (`quest_1` to `quest_8`)
+  - 8 evening/nighttime quest loops (`evening_1` to `evening_8`)
+  - 12 toothbrushing loops (`teeth_1` to `teeth_12`)
+  - 10 victory fanfares (`fanfare_1` to `fanfare_10`)
+- **AppMusicManager Singleton:** Created a centralized `AppMusicManager` class to handle background loop media player creation, category track random selection/rotation on screen entry, volume settings updates, and self-releasing fanfare playback.
+- **Screen Integrations:**
+  - `TodayScreen.kt`: Plays morning quest loops during the day.
+  - `DragonNookScreen.kt`: Plays cozy nook loops.
+  - `ToothbrushingScreen.kt`: Plays teeth brushing loops while the timer runs, pausing/stopping along with the user.
+- **Dynamic Evening Bedtime Music:** Integrated the 8 calming twilight evening quest loops. If the time is past the evening start hour (e.g. 5:00 PM), the Kid TodayScreen dynamically transitions to playing the calming twilight tracks.
+- **Global Volume Slider Links:**
+  - `JarsScreen.kt` was modified to load `sound_volume` and apply it to its Text-to-Speech narration and the `SoundSynth.volumeScale` for button sound effects.
+  - Settings slider adjustments apply to TTS voice, chiptunes, and music loops globally.
+- **Parent Settings Portal Adjustments:** Added an "Evening Music Start Time" configuration block in the parentSettings panel (SettingsScreen.kt) with tactile `+` and `-` buttons. This allows parents to define exactly when the app switches from daytime quest music to sleepy evening quest music.
+
+---

## 🤖 Assistant (2026-06-10 16:54:53 UTC)

I have completed all the tasks for this iteration. Here is a summary of the work done:

### 1. Converted and Integrated Twilight Evening Quest Audio
* Converted the 8 newly provided evening quest `.wav` files (e.g. *Cartridge Lullaby*, *Cinnamon Circuit*, *Cinnamon Tile*, *Milk Cart Silence*) to optimized, gapless `.ogg` loops named `evening_1.ogg` through `evening_8.ogg` in the Android `raw` resource directory.
* Built dynamic, time-of-day-based music routing into `AppMusicManager`. If the current time is past the evening start threshold, `TodayScreen` automatically cycles to a relaxing twilight evening track and loops it. Otherwise, it plays the daytime morning quest tracks.

### 2. Adjustable Evening music Start Time in Parent Settings
* Added the **Evening Music Start Time** setting inside the Parent settings portal (`SettingsScreen.kt` / `SettingsViewModel.kt`).
* Implemented tactile `+` and `-` buttons in the settings dashboard to adjust the evening start hour (ranging from 12:00 PM to 11:00 PM, defaulting to 5:00 PM).
* Connected `TodayScreen` to listen to modifications to this setting so that it immediately recalculates and switches to the correct morning/evening playlist upon returning to the home dashboard.

### 3. Centralized Audio and Global Volume Linking
* Addressed setting linkage across the app by fetching `sound_volume` on the Kid `JarsScreen.kt` and feeding it to its Speech Synthesis narration parameters and the static `SoundSynth.volumeScale` (bubble pop/cash register chime effects).
* Ensured that adjusting the volume slider in the settings updates TTS speech volume, 8-bit chiptune sound effects, and Suno background loops globally.

### 4. Compilation and Delivery
* Ran all automated unit tests (`.\gradlew.bat testDebugUnitTest`), which compiled and passed successfully.
* Compiled the final debug APK containing the new OGG tracks and settings screen updates: `.\gradlew.bat assembleDebug`.
* Copied the resulting package directly to your distribution share: [SophiaQuest.apk](file:///Y:/ReynoldsFamily/builds/SophiaQuest.apk) (new file size: **66.2 MB**).
* Updated the local builds site directory page [index.html](file:///Y:/ReynoldsFamily/builds/index.html) to reflect the new size.
* Registered the music manager, bedtime twilight loops, and adjustable portal features in the local changes history of [sophiaquest-build-dashboard.html](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/ParentingSophia/docs/sophiaquest-build-dashboard.html).
* Ran [sync_logs.ps1](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/ParentingSophia/docs/sync_logs.ps1) to sync the latest conversation transcripts.

## 🤖 Assistant (2026-06-10 16:59:57 UTC)

I have resolved the dialogue-music loudness balance issue. Here is a summary of the additional changes made:

### 1. Dialogue-Music Loudness Balance (dBA)
* **Background Loop Attenuation:** Modified [AppMusicManager.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/ParentingSophia/SophiaQuest/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaquest/utils/AppMusicManager.kt) to scale down the background loop music volume to **30%** (`volume * 0.3f`) of the master volume slider.
* **Master TTS & Chiptunes:** Left the Speech Synthesis (TTS voice) and retro chiptunes (`SoundSynth` button clicks/chimes) at 100% of the master volume. This ensures the background music loops act as a true, gentle backdrop while the TTS voice instructions stand out clearly and are easily understood.

### 2. Verification and Delivery
* Ran all automated unit tests (`.\gradlew.bat testDebugUnitTest`) successfully.
* Recompiled the final package: `.\gradlew.bat assembleDebug`.
* Placed the compiled APK in your builds directory: [SophiaQuest.apk](file:///Y:/ReynoldsFamily/builds/SophiaQuest.apk) (final size: **66.2 MB**).
* Logged the changes and volume attenuation factor in the change notes of [sophiaquest-build-dashboard.html](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/ParentingSophia/docs/sophiaquest-build-dashboard.html).
* Ran [sync_logs.ps1](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/ParentingSophia/docs/sync_logs.ps1) to sync this conversation to your logs directory.

## 🤖 Assistant (2026-06-10 17:13:29 UTC)

The reason it wasn't showing as checked off in Phase 4 of your browser is due to **local browser storage caching**.

The dashboard code is designed to load its progress state from your browser's local cache under the key `'sophiaquest-dashboard-v1'`. Since you had previously loaded the dashboard when the music integration task was not yet complete (and therefore saved as `false` in your browser cache), it was still displaying as unchecked.

To resolve this:
1. **Auto-refresh implemented:** I updated the note for the brushing music task to reflect the Suno OGG integrations and bumped the dashboard state version key from `v1` to `v2`.
2. **Result:** When you refresh the dashboard webpage in your browser, it will now automatically load the fresh, correct defaults and mark the task as checked off.
3. If for any reason it still appears unchecked, you can simply click the **"Reset ticks"** button on the dashboard toolbar to clear your browser's local cache and reload all the correct code-derived progress states.

				5:date: 2026-0608
13:> **Version note (v5):** Incorporates the resolutions from the devil's-advocate audit (see Section 13 for the finding-by-finding map) and a new hard constraint: **no new third-party accounts or SaaS signups.** Key changes from v4: (1) re-scoped to a **lean v1** with an explicit scope statement; (2) **phases renumbered linearly**; (3) **cross-household sync deferred to a fast-follow (v1.1)** so the core loop is validated first — single-household v1 uses offline export/import; (4) **ongoing compounding replaced for v1 by a save-goal bonus** (preserves cause-and-effect); (5) added a **jar-check reconciliation** feature so the digital ledger can't silently drift from the physical cash; (6) **mascot voice = on-device TTS + optional parent-recorded VO**; (7) **local on-device notifications only** (no push); (8) added a **core-loop validation gate**, a **first-run flow**, **kill/pivot criteria**, and a **no-account open-source building-blocks list**. Retained: track-only physical-jar money, $5/week, license-free original mascot (mystical creature / fun animal), OpenArt/Suno asset pipeline, local-first, Pixel 10 Pro XL / Pro targets.
25:Two hard constraints shape every technical choice: **everything is open-source and free,** and **no new third-party accounts or SaaS signups are required** — so the app uses on-device capabilities (Text-to-Speech, local notifications, local storage), open-source libraries, and the stakeholder's existing tools (**OpenArt** for art, **Suno** for music) rather than anything that demands another login.
72:- **No push notifications / no cloud services that require an account.**
152:4. **Notifications** — opt-in **local** reminder for "routine time" (off by default); no push.
157:- Minimum tap target **64dp**; primary kid actions larger; comfortable on the **6.3" Pro** as well as the 6.8" Pro XL.
161:- **Responsive across 6.3" and 6.8" / differe
<truncated 225 bytes>
the weekly "jar check" prompt. No FCM, no account. Honors the "no nagging" stance.
243:- **WorkManager + AlarmManager** — scheduling + **local** notifications (no FCM).
257:- **Pixel 10 Pro XL (Tom):** 6.8" LTPO OLED, 1344x2992, 20:9, 486 ppi, Android 16, Tensor G5.
295:- **Scheduling/notifications:** WorkManager + AlarmManager (local only).
349:### Phase 5 — First-Run, Dashboard, Local Notifications, Polish & v1 Pilot (= v1 release)
351:- **Tasks:** guided **first-run** (incl. seeding jars from current cash + optional VO recording); **parent dashboard** finalize; **opt-in local notifications** (off by default); **backup/restore** (encrypted local export); **accessibility & safe-area audit** on both Pixels; **performance/crash pass**; **diagnostics retirement** (disable/remove logging + screen for the production build; confirm no child-activity logs ship); **content & delight pass**; **single-household pilot with Sophia**; fix top issues. **Test with Sophia throughout.**
397:| Layout breaks across 6.3"/6.8" | Inconsistent UX | Adaptive Compose; density-independent units; test both |
398:| New-account friction | Stalls/annoys builder | **No-account building blocks** (Section 7.7): TTS, local notifications, QR pairing, local encrypted backup |
437:- Notifications: **local on-device only**, opt-in, off by default.
472:| 8 | Notifications unspecified | **Local, opt-in, off-by-default** (Section 6.8); no push/account |

notificationManager.createNotificationChannel(channel)\\n        }\\n\\n        // Tap action\\n        val intent = Intent(applicationContext, MainActivity::class.java).apply {\\n            flags = ## 🤖 Assistant (2026-06-10 17:14:32 UTC)

I will create `NotificationScheduler.kt` to schedule the unique periodic background tasks using WorkManager. This class calculates the initial delays needed to align reminders with 8:00 AM (daily chores) and Sunday 9:00 AM (weekly jar check).


### 🛠️ Tool Executions

<details>
<summary><b>Call `write_to_file` - *"Creating NotificationScheduler.kt"*</b></summary>

Arguments:
```json
{
  "CodeContent": "\"package com.example.sophiaquest.utils\\n\\nimport android.content.Context\\nimport androidx.work.Data\\nimport androidx.work.ExistingPeriodicWorkPolicy\\nimport androidx.work.PeriodicWorkRequestBuilder\\nimport androidx.work.WorkManager\\nimport java.util.Calendar\\nimport java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit\\n\\nobject NotificationScheduler {\\n    private const val DAILY_WORK_NAME = \\\"com.example.sophiaquest.DAILY_REMINDER\\\"\\n    private const val WEEKLY_WORK_NAME = \\\"com.example.sophiaquest.WEEKLY_REMINDER\\\"\\n\\n    fun scheduleReminders(context: Context, dailyEnabled: Boolean, weeklyEnabled: Boolean) {\\n        val workManager = WorkManager.getInstance(context)\\n\\n        // 1. Daily Chores Reminder\\n        if (dailyEnabled) {\\n            val calendar = Calendar.getInstance()\\n            val now = calendar.timeInMillis\\n            calendar.set(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY, 8)\\n            calendar.set(Calendar.MINUTE, 0)\\n            calendar.set(Calendar.SECOND, 0)\\n            if (calendar.timeInMillis <= now) {\\n                calendar.add(Calendar.DAY_OF_YEAR, 1)\\n            }\\n            val initialDelay = calendar.timeInMillis - now\\n\\n            val data = Data.Builder()\\n                .putString(\\\"reminder_type\\\", \\\"DAILY\\\")\\n                .build()\\n\\n            val dailyRequest = PeriodicWorkRequestBuilder<ReminderWorker>(24, TimeUnit.HOURS)\\n                .setInitialDelay(initialDelay, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)\\n                .setInputData(data)\\n                .build()\\n\\n            workManager.enqueueUniquePeriodicWork(\\n                DAILY_WORK_NAME,\\n                ExistingPeriodicWorkPolicy.UPDATE,\\n                dailyRequest\\n            )\\n        } else {\\n            workManager.cancelUniqueWork(DAILY_WORK_NAME)\\n        }\\n\\n        // 2. Weekly Sunday Jar Check Reminder\\n        if (weeklyEnabled) {\\n            val calendar = Calendar.getInstance()\\n            val now = calendar.timeInMillis\\n            calendar.set(Calendar.DAY_OF_WEEK, Calendar.SUNDAY)\\n            calendar.set(Calendar.\n<truncated 932 bytes>",
  "Description": "\"Create NotificationScheduler singleton helper for WorkManager unique periodic reminders setup\"",
  "IsArtifact": "false",
  "Overwrite": "true",

}

+    fun checkAndRequestPermission(onGranted: () -> Unit) {
+        if (android.os.Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= android.os.Build.VERSION_CODES.TIRAMISU) {
+            val permission = android.Manifest.permission.POST_NOTIFICATIONS
+            val check = androidx.core.content.ContextCompat.checkSelfPermission(context, permission)
+            if (check == android.content.pm.PackageManager.PERMISSION_GRANTED) {
+                onGranted()
+            } else {
+                permissionLauncher.launch(permission)
+            }
+        } else {
+            onGranted()
+        }

+                                    if (checked) {
+                                        checkAndRequestPermission {
+                                            viewModel.setWeeklyReminderEnabled(context, true)
+                                        }
+                                    } else {
+                                        viewModel.setWeeklyReminderEnabled(context, false)
+                                    }
+                                }
+                            )
+                        }
+                    }
+                }
+            }
+
             // Category: Jar splits settings
             item {
                 Text(

1: # Implementation Plan: Dragon Hatch Flow & Toothbrushing Parent Bypass
2: 
3: This plan addresses:
4: 1. The bug where the dragon egg reaches the required star count but does not show a hatch celebration UI, causing subsequent stars to be awarded to the next unhatched dragon hidden in the database (which makes the UI appear as if it "lost count of the points through the day").
5: 2. The request for a parent-bypass "Easter Egg" to mark toothbrushing as done without running the full timer, by holding (long-pressing) the dragon mascot on the toothbrushing screen.
6: 
7: ---
8: 
9: ## Proposed Changes
10: 
11: ### Component 1: Today Screen UI & ViewModel
12: 
13: #### [MODIFY] [TodayScreen.kt](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/ParentingSophia/SophiaQuest/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaquest/ui/kid/TodayScreen.kt)
14: - **ViewModel Updates:**
15:   - Update `TodayUiState.Success` signature to include `val activeDragonId: Long`.
16:   - Inside `TodayViewModel.uiState` flow construction, map `activeDragonId = activeDragon?.id ?: 0L` to `TodayUiState.Success`.
17:   - Add method `fun acknowledgeHatch(dragonId: Long)` to `TodayViewModel` that launches a coroutine calling `repository.acknowledgeDragonHatch(dragonId)`.
18: - **UI Screen Updates:**
19:   - If `state.isHatched` is true in `TodayScreen.kt`, replace the standard star progress bar `Card` with a premium **Celebratory Card**:
20:     - Background: Warm light gold/yellow (`0xFFFFF9C4`).
21:     - Title: `"🎉 [state.activeDragonName] Hatched! 🎉"` in dark
<truncated 1232 bytes>
d out status (e.g. `"Brushing teeth!"` or `"Sleeping dragon."`).
38: 
39: ---
40: 
41: ### Component 3: Unit Tests
42:

1: # Walkthrough: Dragon Hatching Flow, Toothbrushing Bypass, & APK Build
2: 
3: We have successfully resolved the dragon egg hatching flow, implemented the parent-bypass shortcut for toothbrushing, verified all unit tests pass, compiled the debug APK, and deployed the build to the local network share and builds website.
4: 
5: ---
6: 
7: ## 1. Summary of Completed Changes
8: 
9: ### Dragon Hatch Acknowledgment Flow (`TodayScreen.kt`)
10: - **State & ViewModel Updates:** Added `activeDragonId` to `TodayUiState.Success`. Implemented `acknowledgeHatch(dragonId: Long)` in `TodayViewModel` which launches a coroutine calling `repository.acknowledgeDragonHatch(dragonId)`.
11: - **Hatch Celebration Card:** If `state.isHatched` is true in `TodayScreen.kt`, the standard star progress bar card is replaced by a bright gold/yellow celebratory card (`0xFFFFF9C4`). This card displays:
12:   - Header: `"🎉 [state.activeDragonName] Hatched! 🎉"` in dark purple.
13:   - Subtext: `"Your baby [state.activeDragonType] is ready to play in the nook!"`.
14:   - Button: `"Get Next Egg! 🥚"` which plays the fanfare chime and triggers `viewModel.acknowledgeHatch(state.activeDragonId)` to transition the active hatching dragon to the next unhatched egg in the database.
15: 
16: ### Toothbrushing Parent Bypass Easter Egg (`ToothbrushingScreen.kt`)
17: - Added a `pointerInput` gesture listener to the toothbrushing mascot `Box`.
18: - **Long Press Gesture:** Parents can press and hold (long-click) the sleeping or brushing dragon mascot to skip the 2-minute timer:
19:
<truncated 1613 bytes>
ng the day.
40:   - `DragonNookScreen.kt`: Plays cozy nook loops.
41:   - `ToothbrushingScreen.kt`: Plays teeth brushing loops while the timer runs, pausing/stopping along with the user.
42: - **Dynamic Evening Bedtime Music:** Integrated the 8 calming twilight evening quest loops. If the time is past the evening start hour (e.g. 5:00 PM), the Kid TodayScreen dynamically transitions to playing the calming twilight tracks.
43: - **Global Volume Slider Links:**
44:   - `JarsScreen.kt` was modified to load `sound_volume` and apply it to its Text-to-Speech narration and the `SoundSynth.volumeScale` for button sound effects.
45:   - Settings slider adjustments apply to TTS voice, chiptunes, and music loops globally.
46: - **Parent Settings Portal Adjustments:** Added an "Evening Music Start Time" configuration block in the parentSettings panel (SettingsScreen.kt) with tactile `+` and `-` buttons. This allows parents to define exactly when the app switches from daytime quest music to sleepy evening quest music.
47: 
48: ---
49: 
50: ## 2. Compilation and Delivery
51: 
52: ### Gradle Compilation
53: - Cleaned the build tree: `.\gradlew.bat clean`.
54: - Ran unit tests: `.\gradlew.bat testDebugUnitTest` (All unit tests passed cleanly).
55: - Built debug APK: `.\gradlew.bat assembleDebug` (Build succeeded).
56:

59: - **Builds Website:** Updated the HTML file [index.html](file:///Y:/ReynoldsFamily/builds/index.html) to show the new APK size of 66.2 MB.
60: - **Build Dashboard:** Prepend new changelog entries and task ticks to [sophiaquest-build-dashboard.html](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/ParentingSophia/docs/sophiaquest-build-dashboard.html).

1: package com.example.sophiaquest.utils
2: 
3: import android.content.Context
4: import androidx.work.Data
5: import androidx.work.ExistingPeriodicWorkPolicy
6: import androidx.work.PeriodicWorkRequestBuilder
7: import androidx.work.WorkManager
8: import java.util.Calendar
9: import java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit
10: 
11: object NotificationScheduler {
12:     private const val DAILY_WORK_NAME = "com.example.sophiaquest.DAILY_REMINDER"
13:     private const val WEEKLY_WORK_NAME = "com.example.sophiaquest.WEEKLY_REMINDER"
14: 
15:     fun scheduleReminders(context: Context, dailyEnabled: Boolean, weeklyEnabled: Boolean) {
16:         val workManager = WorkManager.getInstance(context)
17: 
18:         // 1. Daily Chores Reminder
19:         if (dailyEnabled) {
20:             val calendar = Calendar.getInstance()
21:             val now = calendar.timeInMillis
22:             calendar.set(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY, 8)
23:             calendar.set(Calendar.MINUTE, 0)
24:             calendar.set(Calendar.SECOND, 0)
25:             if (calendar.timeInMillis <= now) {
26:                 calendar.add(Calendar.DAY_OF_YEAR, 1)
27:             }
28:             val initialDelay = calendar.timeInMillis - now
29: 
30:             val data = Data.Builder()
31:                 .putString("reminder_type", "DAILY")
32:                 .build()
33: 
34:             val dailyRequest = PeriodicWorkRequestBuilder<ReminderWorker>(24, TimeUnit.HOURS)
35:                 .setInitialDelay(initialDelay, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)
36:                 .setInputData(data)
37:                 .build()
38: 
39:             workManager.enqueueUniquePeriodicWork(
40:                 DAILY_WORK_NAME,
41:                 ExistingPeriodicWorkPolicy.UPDATE,
42:                 dailyRequest
43:             )
44:         } else {
45:             workManager.cancelUniqueWork(DAILY_WORK_NAME)
46:         }
47: 
48:         // 2. Weekly Sunday Jar Check Reminder
49:         if (weeklyEnabled) {
50:             val calendar = Calendar.getInstance()
51:             val now = calendar.timeInMillis
52:             calendar.set(Calendar.DAY_OF_WEEK, Calendar.SUNDAY)
53:             calendar.set(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY, 9)
54:             calendar.set(Calendar.MINUTE, 0)
55:             calendar.set(Calendar.SECOND, 0)
56:             if (calendar.timeInMillis <= now) {
57:                 calendar.add(Calendar.WEEK_OF_YEAR, 1)
58:             }
59:             val initialDelay = calendar.timeInMillis - now
60: 
61:             val data = Data.Builder()
62:                 .putString("reminder_type", "WEEKLY")
63:                 .build()
64: 
65:             val weeklyRequest = PeriodicWorkRequestBuilder<ReminderWorker>(7, TimeUnit.DAYS)
66:                 .setInitialDelay(initialDelay, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)
67:                 .setInputData(data)
68:                 .build()
69: 
70:             workManager.enqueueUniquePeriodicWork(
71:                 WEEKLY_WORK_NAME,
72:                 ExistingPeriodicWorkPolicy.UPDATE,
73:                 weeklyRequest
74:             )
75:         } else {
76:             workManager.cancelUniqueWork(WEEKLY_WORK_NAME)
77:         }
78:     }
79: }
80:
